Based on this, conclusions are drawn about the characteristics of energy conversion.TasksSupply the pump with electrical energy from a solar battery.Program the Cobra SMARTsense code to monitor the electric current.Investigate how light intensity affects the pump’s performance.Learning ObjectiveStudents learn about the method of pumped-storage power plants, which make it possible to store renewable energy. They are also introduced to basic programming of a device for current monitoring and relay switching.BenefitsTeaching with a focus on the future: Integration of sensors (Cobra SMARTsense) into digital science educationIntroduction to programming using the Cobra SMARTsense codeIllustrates key contemporary topics such as energy conversion and renewable energySafe experimentation: The lamp is protected from direct contact, the housing is well ventilated through drilled openings, and only slightly heats upVersatile power supply: The included power unit is highly adaptable and particularly suitable for student experiments across all age groups

name: Raised bog and fen with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Bogs are sites for plants that specialise in soils with a constant surplus of water. Apart from this common feature, peatlands are very different. For example, raised bogs and fens form different plant communities because of their different living conditions, which we will investigate in this experiment. Benefits experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students measure the pH value and conductivity of the fen and the raised bog and compare the two values. They also pay special attention to the vegetation and describe the differences. Learning Objectives The pupils should recognise that different environmental conditions prevail in the raised and lowland moors and that different plants therefore occur in these moors.

name: Range and shielding of alpha radiation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The range of α rays should be known by the shielding by means of paper, which completely absorbs the α particles and thus causes a reduction of the radiation intensity. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students determine the range of the α rays by shielding the α particles with paper. Learning Objectives The aim of the experiment is to determine the range and shielding of α radiation.

name: Redox Volumetric Titration: Cerimetry with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Potassium permanganate solutions which are used as oxidizing measuring solutions in redox titrations can in most cases be replaced by Ce(IV) solutions. These offer the advantages that they do not change on storage and that the course of the redox titration can be very conveniently followed by measuring the electrochemical potential. The equivalent point can then be found by determination of the inflection point of the potential curve which results from plotting the measured values. Tasks Titrate Iron(II) sulphate solution with Ce(IV) sulphate solution. Learning objectives Redox titration Iron(II) sulphate Ce(IV) sulphate Titration Cerimetry Benefits Drop counter optimizes the experimental procedure Simplified implementation: all pre-settings already prepared Extensive automation of the experiment allows concentration on the learning objectives Necessary accessories Precision balance 6200g/0.01g Software provided. Computer not included.

name: Relationship between voltage and current in conduction processes in liquids with Cobra SMARTsense current in conductive proces-
description: Principle If a voltage is applied to two electrodes immersed in an aqueous solution of an electrolyte, the ions move in the direction of the electrode with the opposite electrical polarity. Aqueous solutions of electrolytes are therefore electrically conductive. Tasks Prepare an aqueous solution of copper sulphate. Investigate the relationship between voltage and current as the current passes through the solution. Learning objectives In the experiment, students should find out whether Ohm's law applies to aqueous solutions. Advantages No additional cable connections required between the components - clearer and faster assembly Contact reliability thanks to interlocking components Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the top and real components on the bottom

name: Respiration and metabolism of warm animals with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The metabolic activity of warm animals depends on the outside temperature. It follows that respiration also decreases at lower temperatures. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Compact, easily transportable experimental set-up suitable for demo experiments and practical training Flexible test execution thanks to wireless data transmission Tasks In this experiment, the pupils and students will measure the metabolic activity as a function of the outside temperature by observing CO2 emissions. It is advisable for the students to bring various animals with them. Learning Objectives In this experiment, the students will study animals at varying temperatures.

name: Respiration of the human being with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle In this experiment, human respiration is illustrated by measurements of the oxygen and carbon dioxide content of ambient air and of repiratory air. Without the conversion of O2 into CO2, human life and the life of many other animals would not exist. Essential for this are also the plants, which convert CO2 into O2 through photosynthesis and thus provide us with a basic building block of our existence. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increased media competence Compact, easily transportable experimental set-up Suitable for demo experiments and practical training Flexible test execution thanks to wireless data transmission Tasks Determination of the oxygen and carbon dioxide concentration once before and once after breathing into a plastic bag Learning objectives Breathing Oxygen concentration in air and breathing air Carbon dioxide in air and breathing air Carbon cycle

name: Salinity changes of a watercourse with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle In this experiment it is shown how the salt leaching of a disused potash dump drastically increases the salt concentration of a stream, how the salt content downstream only gradually decreases again and how the increased salt concentration can still be measured downstream after it flows into a river. Benefits experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks measuring the conductivity of a flowing water at various points compare measured values and make statements about the reasons for the changes Learning Objectives Groundwater salt washout fertilisers anthropogenic influences

name: Salinity of soils and plant substrates with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle How suitable are certain soils and plant substrates for plant nutrition? This experiment is concerned with measuring the conductivity of soils and plant substrates that have already been tested for the presence of nutrients for plant growth. It is also important to find out which of the soils and plant substrates already contain nutrients for plant growth and to which it is better to add plant nutrients in the form of fertilisers. Plants obtain their nutrients in the form of ions from salts in the soil. The most important of the 16 nutrients that plants need for growth are nitrogen in the form of nitrates and ammonium, phosphorus in the form of phosphates and potassium in the form of potassium salts. In addition, soils need lime (calcium carbonate) to prevent soil acidification (pH reduction). For agricultural use, these nutrients are added to the soil via inorganic fertilizers. Potting soil added with fertilizers is used for indoor plants. Benefits Experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile and desktop use Tasks Selection of suitable samples Preparing samples for analysis Determination of the conductivity of the samples Comparison of the measured values of the samples Learning objectives Salt content Soil samples Planting substrates Fertilisers

name: Series and parallel connection of sources of voltage with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle In a series connection, the voltages of the batteries add up, while the current remains essentially the same. In a parallel connection, the voltage stays the same as with a single battery, but more current can be delivered or the energy can be provided for a longer time. This experiment investigates this difference by measuring voltage and current. Tasks First, build a simple circuit with one battery and measure the voltage and the current. Extend the circuit to a series connection of two batteries and measure the voltage and the current again. Now build a parallel connection of two batteries and measure the voltage and the current. Learning objective Students investigate how voltage and current change when two batteries are connected in series or in parallel. Benefits High-contrast printing of electrical symbols on the top side. Minimal preparation time and easy execution of the experiment thanks to digital experiment instructions.

name: Set Gas laws with glass jacket and Cobra SMARTSense
description: Function and Applications All gas laws can be measured with an experimental setup - The composition of a gas depends on its temperature, its pressure and the mole fraction. For the limiting case of an ideal gas, these state variables are linked by the general equation of state, from which special relationships for certain changes in state can be derived. This set is a complete set of devices for working out the gas laws experimentally with the Cobra SMARTsense absolute pressure sensors and the thermocouple, as well as the glass jacket system.   Benefits Compact system - can be stored easily when assembled, ready for measurement within minutes  Didactic setup: The relationship between pressure, volume, temperature is directly visible, all parameters are measured Very comfortable and reliable temperature control due to water bath The parameters are measured with a PC and can therefore easily be demonstrated, displayed graphically and analyzed Optional accessories: Digital large scale display for demonstration experiments in the classroom or lecture hall   Equipment and Technical Data Glass jacket Gas syringe for glass jacket system Heating device for glass jacket system Immersion sensor Cobra SMARTsense Absolute Pressure sensor Cobra SMARTsense Thermocouple sensor Other accessories and stand material for carrying out the three gas laws of Gay-Lussac, Amontons and Boyle

name: Solar ray collector with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿ Principle The solar ray collector is illuminated with a high intensity lamp of known light intensity. The heat energy absorbed by the collector can be calculated from the volume flow and the difference in the water temperatures at the inlet and outlet of the absorber, if the inlet temperature stays almost constant by releasing energy to a reservoir. The efficiency of the collector is determined from this. The measurement is made with various collector arrangements and at various absorber temperatures. Benefits Complete solution to study all aspects of thermal energy using a solar ray collector Covers one of the most important and highly visible technologies in renewable energy Tasks To determine the efficiency of the solar ray collector under various experimental conditions. Absorption of energy from the environment (20 °C) without illumination by sun or halogen lamp, water temperature at the absorber inlet T Illumination with high intensity lamp. Water temperature T Illumination with high intensity lamp. Water temperature T Learning objectives Absorption Heat radiation Greenhouse effect Convection Conduction of heat Collector equations Efficiency Energy ceiling

name: Solubility product with Cobra SMARTsense
subcategory: Mixtures Of Substances Separation Of Substances
description: Principle The solubility of poorly soluble salts is expressed as the solubility product, i.e. the product of the concentration of cations and anions in the solution which are in equilibrium with the solid salt. These concentrations can be determined via conductivity measurements. Benefits Good and reproducible results due to temperature control in the experiment. The use of the compact Cobra4 Mobile-Link is space-saving. Tasks Measure the conductivities of saturated aqueous solutions of the salts calcium fluoride and calcium carbonate at 25 °C. With the aid of tabulated ionic conductivities, calculate the solubility products of the salts from their conductivities. Learning objectives Solubility Dissociation Electrolytic conductance Activity Necessary accessories Precision balance 620g/0.001g Software provided. Computer not included.

name: Solution pressure - reactivity of precious and base metals with Cobra SMARTsense
description: (no description provided)

name: Specific heat capacity of metals with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Calorimetry can be used to determine the heat content of a piece of metal. The heat content can be calculated from the mixing temperature set in the calorimeter. Benefits Particularly understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increase of media competence Tasks The students heat metal blocks of the same mass but of different types of metal in a water bath to the same temperature and then bring them individually into a water-filled calorimeter with a known starting temperature. They then measure the mixing temperature and calculate the specific heat capacity of the individual metal samples. Learning objectives The students learn how to measure specific heat capacities of metals with a calorimeter.

name: Storage of the electric energy from a solar cell in a capacitor with Cobra SMARTsense Code
description: Principle A capacitor is a passive electric component with the ability to store electrical charge and so the corresponding energy. Benefits Experiment is part of a complete solution set with a total of 26 experiments for Renewable Energy solar cells, wind power, water power Safe experimentation: the lamp is protected against touch, the housing is well ventilated with the aid of holes and is only slightly heated Doubled learning success: Electric circuit diagram on top, real components can be seen at the bottom Tasks How can solar energy be stored without using a rechargeable battery? Try to store the energy produced by a solar battery in a capacitor.

name: Storing wind and solar energy in a battery with Cobra SMARTsense Code
description: PrincipleIn this experiment, wind energy and solar energy are stored in a rechargeable battery. When energy production becomes too low, the battery uses its stored energy to power a lamp, demonstrating how stored energy can be used to cover energy demand during periods of insufficient generation.TasksCharge a rechargeable battery using solar and wind energy.Power an incandescent lamp using the stored energy when insufficient energy is being produced.Program the switching processes with Cobra SMARTsense Code so that they operate automatically.Learning objectiveLearners discover that surplus energy from renewable sources can be stored and used again at a later time. They are also introduced to simple programming of a device for voltage and current monitoring as well as relay switching.BenefitsProgramming of the Code Actuator using the Blockly programming language or a trigger function.Contact-safe design through puzzle-like interlocking modules.Use of standard Cobra SMARTsense sensors for teaching in schools and universities.Safe experimentation: the lamp is protected against accidental contact, the housing is well ventilated through ventilation openings, and it heats up only slightly.The power supply used is versatile and particularly suitable for student experiments at all educational levels ("RiSU 2016 compliant").

name: Substrate inhibition of enzymes with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The enzymatic hydrolysis of urea in aqueous solution yields carbon dioxide and ammonia. The ions of these compounds increase the conductivity of the solution. Conductivity measurements can be used to determine the rates of urea hydrolysis by the enzyme urease at different substrate concentrations. Excessive substrate concentrations lead to substrate inhibition. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increased media competence Compact, easily transportable experimental set-up Suitable for demo experiments and practical training Flexible test execution thanks to wireless data transmission Tasks Measurement of conductivity and determination of the rate of urea hydrolysis by the enzyme urease at different substrate concentrations Learning objectives Substrate inhibition Urea enzymolysis Conductivity diagram Enzyme hydrolysis reaction rate

name: Superposition of two magnetic fields of coils with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The superposition of electric fields is usually known. Magnetic fields also overlap. In this experiment this superposition is investigated. The Helmholtz coil is an example where two magnetic fields of two coils overlap. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks measuring the magnetic flux density of two coils. The coils have the same polarity so that the magnetic fields complement each other positively. measuring the magnetic flux density along two coils. The polarity of one coil is reversed so that the magnetic fields between the coils are superimposed negatively. Learning Objectives In this experiment the magnetic flux density of two coils is to be measured. The superposition of two fields is investigated. In the first part the two magnetic fields are in the same direction and in the second part they are in opposite directions.

name: Switching on coils with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle In this experiment, self-induction is investigated - a property of coils in which a voltage is generated by changing the current. This counteracts the change in current and leads to a time delay when switching on and off. Tasks What effect does a coil have when closing a DC circuit? Investigate the voltage across a resistor connected in series with a coil. Learning objective The aim of the experiment is to learn that when a coil is switched on, a self-induction voltage is created which counteracts the increase in current. Advantages Simple setup in just a few minutes. High resolution data acquisition (up to 10 kHz). Improved media competence. Safe student power supply (RiSU-compliant).

name: Temperature dependence of conductivity with Cobra SMARTsenseuctivity with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The electrical conductivity of an electrolytic solution is dependent not only upon the type and concentration of the electrolytes, but also other state values. Thus, an increase in conductivity is generally observed with an increase in temperature. This is fundamentally due to the exponential decrease of the solutions's viscosity. In aqueous solutions a limit is reached at approximately 90 °C. Above this temperature the conductivity again decreases. Benefits Experimental procedure without dangerous chemicals Simplified implementation: all pre-settings already prepared Accurate results by conductivity probe with integrated sensor Pt 1000 Tasks Determine the temperature dependence of the conductivity of a 10% sodium chloride solution from 20 °C to approximately 60 °C. Learning objectives Electrolytic resistance Conductance Specific and molar conductivity Ion mobility Equivalent conductance at infinite dilution Kohlrausch's law Ostwald's law of dilution Transference numbers Viscosity Necessary accessories Precision balance 620g/0.001g

name: Temperature regulation with Cobra SMARTsense Code
description: ﻿PrincipleExperimental set-up for regulating the temperature of a Peltier element. In the experiment a drink is cooled at a constant temperature.BenefitsProgramming of the controller with Blockly or  a trigger function.Use of standard Cobra SMARTsense sensors for teaching in schools and universities.TasksDetermine the temperature and programme the regulation of a Peltier element.Learning objectivesThe students learn how to create a temperature controller with just a few components.Programming with Blockly.

name: Terrain mapping with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The drawing of maps is very popular with students in geography lessons and is of great didactic value. For example, the local altitude can also be determined by the changing air pressure, which decreases with increasing altitude. Benefits experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increased media competence Tasks Determination of the height of prominent points Create contour lines in a simple map based on the height determination of the individual measuring points Learning Objectives Drawing maps with contour lines

name: The Bergmann rule: heat loss as a function of the body surface area and volume with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The anatomist and physiologist Carl Bergmann established this rule, named after him, in 1847: due to the heat radiation on the surface of warm-blooded animals, one often encounters the larger species or breeds in colder areas and the smaller ones in warmer areas. In this experiment, the ratio of volume to body surface and its cooling is determined using a model with differently sized, heated potatoes. The latest digital measuring technology is used here, which makes this rule very clear and easy for pupils to understand. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increased media competence Tasks Students should use the Cobra SMARTsense Temperature to record the temperature in two differently sized heated potatoes over a period of 15 minutes. Learning objectives Students should recognize that a large body loses less heat than a small body in the same time and under the same climatic conditions.

name: The NTC resistor with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The students already know that metallic conductors have a resistance that increases as the temperature rises. Now they should recognise the opposite behaviour of NTC resistors (Negative Temperature Coefficient), whose resistance decreases when heated. The experiment consists of two parts: Part 1 (self-heating) serves as an introduction, part 2 (external heating) as a confirmation experiment.  Tasks Investigate the relationship between temperature and resistance in an NTC resistor. Learning objectives Using the experiment, learners recognise how an NTC resistor works. Benefits No additional cable connections required between the components - clearer and faster assembly Contact reliability thanks to interlocking components Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the top and real components on the bottom

name: The PTC resistor with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle A PTC resistor or PTC thermistor (positive temperature coefficient thermistor) is a temperature-dependent resistor that belongs to the group of thermistors. Its main characteristic is a positive temperature coefficient, therefore conducting electricity better at low temperatures than at high temperatures. Tasks Build a circuit with a PTC resistor. Heat the resistor using their own heat and external heating. Determine the resistance values at different currents and temperatures. Learning objectives The students understand that the resistance of a PTC resistor increases with increasing temperature. They learn the difference between self-heating and external heating and deepen their understanding of the temperature dependence of the PTC resistor. Advantages No additional cable connections required between the components - clearer and faster assembly Contact reliability thanks to interlocking components Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the top side and real components on the bottom side

name: The back-scattering of beta radiation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The intensity of the backscattering of β rays is investigated as a function of different materials. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students investigate the backscattering of beta rays by detecting the scattering rate on different materials using the Geiger-Müller counter tube. Learning Objectives With the help of the experiment, the students determine the dependence of the intensity of the backscattering on the material used.

name: The background effect with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Radioactive radiation pulses are registered with the help of the Geiger-Müller counter tube. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The pupils should check whether the Geiger-Müller counter registers pulses even though there is no radioactive source in the classroom. Learning objectives This experiment should enable the students to independently come to the conclusion that radioactivity is a natural process.

name: The behaviour of gamma radiation in a magnetic field with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The behaviour of gamma rays in the magnetic field is investigated by comparing measurements with and without magnetic field. A lead plate is used to isolate other types of radiation. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students study the deflection of gamma rays in a magnetic field by measuring the pulse rate with the Geiger-Müller counter tube for radiation through a magnetic field, as well as without a magnetic field. Learning Objectives With the help of the experiment, the students determine the behaviour of the gamma rays in the magnetic field.

name: The bimetallic switch with Cobra SMARTsense Code
description: Principle A bimetallic strip is a metal strip made of two adjacent metals with different coefficients of thermal expansion. When the bimetal is heated, it bends to one side. This means that bimetals can also be used as switches. The programmable actuator Cobra SMARTsense Code can be used to indicate when a bimetal switch closes or opens the circuit. Tasks Build a bimetal switch into a circuit consisting of a current sensor and resistor so that it can open or close the circuit. Program the code so that a sad smiley appears as soon as the bimetal switch bends due to heat. Learning objectives The students should use the experiment to understand the principle and function of a bimetal switch. They will also learn how to program an actuator with simple "If... then..." logic operations. Advantages No additional cable connections required between the components - clearer and faster assembly Contact reliability thanks to components that can be interlocked like a puzzle Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the top and real components on the bottom

name: The copper/zinc cell (Daniell cell) - Leerlaufspannung eines galvanischen Elements with Cobra SMARTsense
description: (no description provided)

name: The deflection of beta radiation in a magnetic field with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle An angular scale is used to investigate the deflection of beta rays in the magnetic field. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students investigate the deflection of beta rays in the magnetic field by moving the Geiger-Müller counter tube on the angular scale of the mounting plate and detecting the pulse rate. Learning Objectives In this experiment, various findings can be conveyed: β rays consist of electrically charged particles because they are deflected by a magnet. Since the direction of the deflection is opposite to the direction expected according to the three-finger rule, β particles have a negative charge. The stronger the magnetic field, the greater the deflection. When the field direction is changed, the deflection is also in the opposite direction. The β particles have different energy values because they are deflected to different degrees.

name: The density of solid bodies by measuring the buoyancy with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿Principle Pressure differences at the top and bottom of bodies immersed in a medium result in net forces on the body, which reduce the weight force within the medium. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks Determine the buoyancy of bodies from the difference of their weight forces in air and water. Calculate the density of bodies from their weight in air and the volume of displaced water. Learning Objectives Students should understand how static buoyancy affects the body. The apparent reduction in weight under water can be used to determine the volume and density of an unknown body. The underlying relationships should be understood both physically and mathematically.

name: The diode as an electrical valve with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle A diode allows current to pass only in one direction and blocks it in the other. Therefore, it acts like an electric valve. A diode can also be used to pump charge into a capacitor with alternating current. Benefits Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life, etc.) incl. protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation among students through use of the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media literacy. No additional cable connections between the modules necessary - clearer and faster set-up. contact reliability due to puzzle-like interlocking building blocks Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the upper side and real components on the lower side Tasks Investigate what effect a diode has on the charge of a capacitor in an AC circuit. Learning Objectives In this experiment, students should realize that the diode can be used as an electrical valve in circuits.

name: The falling magnet with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle In this experiment, the influence of a changing magnetic field on an electrical conductor is investigated. For this purpose, a magnet is dropped through a coil. Tasks Drop the magnet through the coil and plot the voltage curve. Find out how the induced voltage depends on the drop height of the magnet. Learning objectives Students learn the principle of electromagnetic induction. They examine and understand the course of the induced voltage during the falling time of the magnet. Advantages Simple setup in just a few minutes. High resolution data acquisition (up to 10 kHz). Improved media competence. Safe student power supply (RiSU-compliant).

name: The heat effect of electric current with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The students examine the behaviour of a thin, conductive wire in an electric circuit, observe that the wire heats up due to the current flow and draw the conclusion that electric current can be used for heating, but also that heating is an undesired secondary effect of electric conductors. Benefits Experiment guides are very easy to understand, especially for younger students Associated competences are directly linked to the experiments Curriculum compatible Fit for the future: implementation of tablets possible

name: The influence of quantity on the intensity of radiation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The students use the Geiger-Müller counter tube to investigate the dependence of the radiation intensity on the amount of potassium chloride. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students examine the radiation intensity when the amount of potassium chloride is increased. Learning Objectives The students use the experiment to explain the relationship between the intensity of radiation and the amount of radioactive substance.

name: The internal resistance of a voltage source with Cobra SMARTsenseoltage source
description: Principle A commercially available dry battery or mono cell is well suited for investigating the internal resistance of voltage sources. Its internal resistance is high enough to be easily measurable and it is easy to replace if it is carelessly destroyed by prolonged overloading. Tasks Set up an electric circuit with different resistors and measure the current I and the terminal voltage Uc Determine the internal resistance Ri of the voltage source by evaluating the measurement data. Learning objectives Students recognize that real voltage sources have an internal resistance and learn to determine this experimentally by measuring the current and clamping voltage. Advantages No additional cable connections required between the components - clearer and faster assembly Contact reliability thanks to components that can be interlocked like a puzzle Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: Electrical circuit diagram visible on the top and real components on the bottom

name: The lead accumulator with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The storage of electrical energy is a significant problem for the supply of energy, particularly as the alternating current made available by power stations cannot be directly stored. Direct current, however, can be stored by converting the electrical energy into chemical energy. The device which is suitable for this is called an accumulator, or storage battery. In practice, several accumulator cells are connected together in series to a battery. The students are familiar with such accumulators. This experiment is intended to clearly explain the construction principle and the mode of action of a lead accumulator. Benefits No additional cable connections between the building blocks needed - clear arragned and quick setup Contact saftey due to puzzle blocks system with corrosion-free gold plated contacts Doubled earning sucess: Electric circuit diagram on top, real components can be seen unterside Future-oriented teaching: Integration of sensors (Cobra SMARTsense) into digital science education Tasks How does a lead accumulator function? Demonstrate how electrical energy can be stored chemically by using, as example, a model of a lead accumulator.

name: The magnetic permeability with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The magnetic permeability of an iron core ensures that the magnetic flux density of a current carrying coil increases significantly. This difference can be quantitatively determined with the aid of a magnetic field sensor. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks measure the magnetic flux density of two coils at direct current with and without iron core as a function of the current intensity. Calculate the magnetic permeability of the iron core. measure the magnetic flux density of two coils in alternating current with and without iron core. Calculate the magnetic permeability and compare the result with the first task. Learning Objectives The magnetic permeability is measured quantitatively. The resulting results for direct current and alternating current will be compared.

name: The main shutter motor with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The field of the stator of an electric motor can be generated by an electromagnet, which is then called an excitation coil. In the main shunt motor, the excitation coil is connected in series with the rotor's armature winding. This type of motor is sometimes called a universal motor. Benefits Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life, etc.) incl. protocol questions. Teach in a future-oriented way: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation among students through use of the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media literacy. No additional cable connections between the modules necessary - clearer and faster set-up. contact reliability due to puzzle-like interlocking building blocks Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the upper side and real components on the lower side Tasks Investigate the behavior of the motor model when an electromagnet is placed on it, which is connected in series with the rotor coil. Learning Objectives By means of this experiment, the students should learn about the properties of a main shunt motor, also called a series motor.

name: The pH value of various soils with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Knowledge of the soil is of great importance for agriculture. In order to familiarize the students with this topic, two sub-experiments are used to show, on the basis of pH determinations, how different soils are (sub-experiment "Characteristic pH values of soils") and how great the differences are even within a soil (sub-experiment "Soil profile"). Benefits Experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile and desktop use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Tasks Taking soil samples Identifying soil profiles Taking pH measurements of soil horizons of given soil profiles Learning objectives Soil fertility Soil profile Soil horizon Soil sample pH determination Buffer capacity of soils

name: The permanent magnet DC motor with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Electric motors for not too high power and simple dynamos are often based on the principle that an electromagnet rotates in the field of one (or more) permanent magnets. Benefits Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared description of experiments (reference to everyday life, etc.) incl. protocol questions. Teach in a future-oriented way: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation among students through use of the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media literacy. No additional cable connections between the modules necessary - clearer and faster set-up. contact reliability due to puzzle-like interlocking building blocks Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the upper side and real components on the lower side Tasks Investigate the behavior of the motor model on which a permanent magnet is placed. Learning Objectives With the aid of the model of a permanent magnet DC motor, the students should learn about the basic design and operation of an electric motor.

name: The potentiometer with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Potentiometers are hidden behind many knobs on electrical devices. A potentiometer acts as a voltage divider. It corresponds to a series connection of two variable resistors with center tap, whose resistance values added always have the same sum. Benefits Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life etc.) incl. protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation among students through use of the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media literacy. No additional cable connections between the modules necessary - clearer and faster set-up. contact safety due to puzzle-like interlocking building blocks Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the upper side and real components on the lower side Tasks Investigate the behavior of a potentiometer using a functional model and a rotary potentiometer as commonly used in engineering. Learning Objectives The students should understand the principle of a potentiometer using a model and experience its function in a vivid manner using a technical potentiometer.

name: The potentiometer with Cobra SMARTsense Code
description: Principle The potentiometer is used to set a variable resistance. This is detected by measuring the current in series and the voltage in parallel to the potentiometer. A threshold value is programmed for the Cobra SMARTsense Code at which the system reacts to a certain resistance. Tasks Measure the resistance, voltage and current with varying resistance. Program the Cobra SMARTsense Code. Learning objectives Students should become confident in working with potentiometers and programming the Cobra SMARTsense Code. Ohm's law is illustrated with the help of the calculated resistance as a channel. Advantages Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared experiment description (reference to everyday life etc.) incl. protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased student motivation through use of the intuitive measureAPP. Increase in media skills. No additional cable connections required between the components - clearer and faster assembly Contact reliability thanks to interlocking components Hard gold-plated, corrosion-resistant contacts Double learning success: electrical circuit diagram visible on the top and real components on the bottom

name: The series motor (DEMO) with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle An electromotor can also be operated with an electromagnet as a field magnet. If armature coils and field coils are connected in series, then this is called a series-wound motor. The properties of this motor are studied by observing the direction of rotation and measuring the electric current. Benefits Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared description of experiments (reference to everyday life, etc.) incl. protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons Increased motivation among students through use of the intuitive measureAPP Increased media literacy Demonstrative experiment due to large instruments Easy and sturdy setup

name: The shunt motor (DEMO) with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle An electromotor can also be operated with an electromagnet as a field magnet. If armature coils are connected parallel to the field coils, then this is called a shunt-wound motor. The properties of this motor are studied by observing the direction of rotation and measuring the electric current. Benefits Particularly comprehensible and didactically prepared description of experiments (reference to everyday life, etc.) incl. protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons Increased motivation among students through use of the intuitive measureAPP Increased media literacy Demonstrative experiment due to large instruments Easy and sturdy setup

name: The simple electrical circuit with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿Principle The students should first perceive that an electric current can only flow in a closed circuit. To achieve this, the simplest possible circuit is used, in which there is neither a series or parallel connection, and which is therefore called the simple circuit. The students should memorise which components or elements were used, their functions and the symbols to represent them.   Tasks Build an electric circuit with a light bulb. Examine the function of each component and how they interact. Integrate the current sensor into the circuit and analyze the current flow.   Learning objectives The students should first recognise that an electric current can only flow in a closed circuit. The simplest of all circuits is used for this purpose. They should memorise the components used and their circuit symbols as well as their function and be able to build circuits according to circuit diagrams or reproduce experimental setups using circuit diagrams from the beginning of electricity theory.   Benefits Contact saftey due to puzzle blocks system with corosion-free gold plated contacts Doubled earning success: Electric circuit diagram on top, real components can be seen unterside Future-oriented teaching: Integration of sensors (Cobra SMARTsense) into digital science education

name: Thermal equilibrium with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle If two bodies of different temperature are brought into contact, a temperature equalization takes place. This is also the reason why coffee or tea, for example, cool down to the ambient temperature over time. Benefits Particularly understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increase of media competence Tasks The students observe how temperatures change when a vessel with cold water is dipped into a warm water bath. Learning objectives The students should notice that the greater the temperature difference, the greater the temperature changes.

name: Thermal insulation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The heat conduction from a body to the environment can be reduced by insulating materials. The heat conduction happens by passing on the movements of the molecule, so that high-density materials usually conduct the heat better, i.e. are poor insulating materials. Benefits Particularly understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increase of media competence Tasks The students compare the cooling of water in a simple beaker and in an insulated calorimeter. Learning objectives The students should observe that the cooling process of hot water can be slowed down by insulation.

name: Thermoregulation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle In biology, thermoregulation is understood as the more or less great independence of an organism's body temperature from the outside world. The human body has various functions to regulate the body temperature. Significant interactions with the ambient temperature occur by increasing or decreasing the breathing and heart rate, but also by processes such as sweating. In particular, however, different areas of the body are supplied with blood at different rates. Thus, parts of the body further away from the heart are generally colder. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks Pupils should use the Cobra SMARTsense Temperature to analyse the temperature of different parts of the body. Learning Objectives The students should understand that different parts of the body have different temperatures and that this is an essential part of the body's thermoregulation.

name: Thread oscillation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿Principle By plucking a rope, vibrations are excited on it. These are acoustically perceptible, similar to a guitar, and cause a periodic change in force at the suspension point of the rope. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks Stimulate vibrations on the thread by hand and perceive them acoustically for different rope tensions. Measure with the help of the force sensor the vibration of the thread for different tensions. Learning objectives The students should excite vibrations on a thread and learn how the period and frequency depend on the tension in the thread.

name: Titration curves and buffering capacity with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿ Principle pH values can be measured with the aid of electrochemical measurements and proton-sensitive electrodes (e.g. glass electrodes). By combining a glass electrode with a reference electrode in one housing, a single-rod glass electrode, which is appropriate for acid-base titrations, is created. The titration curves allow an exact determination of the equivalence point in titrations of strong and weak acids and bases. Benefits Drop counter optimizes the experimental procedure Simplified implementation: all pre-settings already prepared Extensive automation of the experiment allows concentration on the learning objectives Tasks Determine the titration curves of different neutralisation reactions. Determine the titration curve of an ampholyte (glycine). Determine the buffering capacity of various aqueous acetic acid/sodium acetate mixtures at different total concentrations.   Learning objectives Strong and weak electrolytes Hydrolysis Dissociation of water Amphoteric electrolytes Isoelectric point Law of mass action Indicators Glass electrode Activity coefficient Buffering capacity Henderson-Hasselbalch equation Necessary accessories Precision balance 6200g/0.01g Software provided. Computer not included.

name: Titration of a polyprotic acid with a strong base with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Acid-base titration represents an analytical procedure for determining concentrations of corresponding compounds. The use of pH measuring electrodes also offers the possibility of generating measurement curves. Potentiometric titrations represent two fundamental laws, the law of mass action (MWG) and Nernst's equation. In this experiment, students record a trace for the titration of a polyvalent acid (phosphoric acid) with a strong base (sodium hydroxide solution). They then identify the characteristic features of such a titration curve and determine the equivalence points. Benefits Experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments from the field of titration and ion detection, simple structure of a teaching series In addition to the graphical display of the measured data on the measuring instrument, all data can also be transferred to a computer for evaluation Experimental literature available for students and teachers: minimum preparation time Risk assessment available for students and teachers Easy teaching and efficient learning when using the available interactive experimental literature

name: Titration of a polyvalent acid with a strong base with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿ Principle Phosphoric acid and sodium hydroxide are to be used to give an example of a titration of a polyvalent acid with a strong base. Benefits Drop counter optimizes the experimental procedure Simplified implementation: all pre-settings already prepared Extensive automation of the experiment allows concentration on the learning objectives Tasks Measure the alteration of the pH value during a titration of an aqueous solution of phosphoric acid with a 0.1 molar sodium hydroxide solution at constant temperature using the Cobra4 system. From the titration curve read the equivalence points and pKa values. Determine the concentration of the acid. Learning objectives Phosphoric acid Sodium hydroxide Polyvalent acid pKa values Proteolysis pH value Necessary accessories Precision balance 620g/0.001g

name: Titration of a strong acid with a strong base using various indicators with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Acid-base titration with the aid of the Cobra SMARTsense pH is used in analytical chemistry for the preliminary examination of corresponding solutions. With its help, statements can be made about the concentration of the substance under investigation. In this experiment, the students determine the concentration of a hydrochloric acid solution with the help of the Cobra SMARTsense pH. To do this, a known volume of this acid is titrated with a volume of a sodium hydroxide solution of known concentration. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is then calculated from the consumed volume of the titrated solution and its concentration. The students should be shown by way of example the use of a pH meter in analytical chemistry. Learning objectives Titration of a strong acid    Benefits Easy digital pH value determination with the aid of the Cobra SMARTsense pH Experimental literature available for pupils and teachers: Minimal preparation time Easy teaching and efficient learning when using the available interactive experimental literature

name: Titration of a strong acid with a strong base with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Acid-base titration represents an analytical procedure for determining concentrations of corresponding compounds. The use of pH measuring electrodes also offers the possibility of creating measurement curves. In this experiment, students record a measurement curve for the titration of a strong acid (hydrochloric acid) with a strong base (sodium hydroxide solution). In doing so, they will then identify the characteristic features of such a titration curve and determine the equivalence point. In the process, the students learn, among other things, that for titrations with strong bases and acids, equivalence point = neutral point. At the equivalence point, the base and the acid are present in equal proportions. The following applies: n(acid) = n(base) This statement can be used to calculate the concentration of the acid. Benefits The experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments from the field of titration and ion detection, and can be used as part of a teaching unit In addition to the graphical representation of the measurement data on the measuring instrument, all data can also be transferred to a computer for evaluation Experimental literature available for students and teachers: minimum preparation time Risk assessment available for students and teachers Easy teaching and efficient learning when using the available interactive experimental literature

name: Titration of a weak acid with a weak base with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle in this experiment, the concentration of an acetic acid solution is determined using a potentiometric titration. In doing so, the students examine the recorded titration curve for characteristic features and determine the equivalence point via a suitable indicator (here: phenolphthalein). Benefits Experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments from the field of titration and ion detection, simple structure of a teaching series In addition to the graphical display of the measurement data on the measuring instrument, all data can also be transferred to a computer for evaluation Experimental literature available for students and teachers: minimum preparation time Risk assessment available for students and teachers Easy teaching and efficient learning when using the available interactive experimental literature

name: Transformer with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The transformer usually consists of two or more coils of insulated copper wire wound on a common iron core. The transformer changes the input AC voltage applied to one coil into the output AC voltage which can be tapped at the other coil. The ratio between input and output voltage corresponds to the ratio of the number of turns of the primary and secondary coils. Benefits Simple design of all components so that students can easily see their function The functional models can be built up successively, thus the functional relationships between the individual components are well understood. No tools necessary Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks In this experiment, the students are to create a transformer from two resting electromagnets (coils) connected by an iron core and to study and learn about the properties of this transformer. Learning Objectives This experiment is to demonstrate the principle and one of the benefits of a transformer.

name: Variable g pendulum with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Earth's gravitational acceleration g is determined for different lengths of the pendulum by means of the oscillating period. If the oscillating plane of the pendulum is not parallel to the gravitational field of the earth, only one component of the gravitational force acts on the pendulum movement. Tasks Determination of the oscillation period of a thread pendulum as a function of the pendulum length. Determination of g. Determination of the gravitational acceleration as a function of the inclination of the pendulum force. Learning objectives Oscillation period Harmonic oscillation Mathematical pendulum Physical pendulum Variable g-pendulum Decomposition of force Gravitational force

name: Ventilation control with Cobra SMARTsense Code
description: Principle Experiment for programming a ventilation control system.   Benefits Programming the controller with the Scratch programming language or with the trigger function. Use of standard Cobra SMARTsense sensors for teaching at schools and universities.   Tasks Determination of the temperature and programming ventilation using a fan. Comparison of the temperature curves of two cooling liquids of the same volume, one with and one without cooling by a fan.   Learning objectives Students learn how to set up a ventilation control system with just a few components.

name: Voltage in a series connection with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The experiment serves as an introduction to the topic and should help the students to understand the function of a series resistor. This motivates them to deal with the law of voltage in series circuits. Tasks Investigate the voltage distribution across resistors in a series circuit by measuring the partial and total voltage. Derive the relationship between the partial voltages across the resistors and the total voltage in an unbranched circuit. Learning objective Students should understand the physical laws and relationships that explain the behavior of electrical voltage in series circuits with resistors. Benefits No additional cable connections between the building blocks needed - clear arragned and quick setup Contact saftey due to puzzle blocks system Corrosion-free gold plated contacts Doubled earning sucess: Electric circuit diagram on top, real components can be seen unterside

name: Voltage measurement with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle A simple circuit with a light bulb is set up. The voltage across the bulb is measured at different supply voltages. Afterwards, the voltage is measured in series with the bulb to clearly demonstrate that voltage must always be measured in parallel. Tasks Set up the circuit according to the circuit diagram. Connect the Cobra SMARTsense Voltage Sensor to the measureAPP and measure the voltage across the bulb. Repeat the measurement at different voltages and then in a series connection to observe the change in voltage. Learning objective Students learn how to measure voltage correctly and understand that the measured value may differ from the supply voltage. Benefits High-contrast printing of electrical symbols on the top surface. Minimal preparation time and easy execution of the experiment thanks to digital experiment instructions.

name: Water quality - contamination by heavy metals with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle Heavy metal ions can get into surface or ground water from deposits or through the waste water of galvanic or similar production plants. They block the action of enzymes so that the metabolic processes controlled by these enzymes can be disturbed or blocked by organisms. With the help of urea hydrolysis, the blockage of enzymes by heavy metal ions can be measured with conventional laboratory methods. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (reference to everyday life etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks The students investigate two enzymatic cleavage processes, one with and one without the addition of heavy metals. Learning Objectives The students should recognize that heavy metals can influence the effect of enzymes.

name: We determine our heart frequency with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle With the help of an electrocardiogram (ECG) you can follow the regular sequence of heart contractions and determine the heart rate by determining the time interval between two heart contractions. In a second attempt, the heart rate of test persons of different ages is measured and it can be shown that, as the human body grows, its average heart rate changes at rest. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks Prepare an electrocardiogram of your heartbeat at rest and calculate your heart rate at rest Describe the age-related differences in heart rate and compare it to your own heart rate at rest Learning Objectives Electrocardiogram Heartbeat heart rate resting pulse

name: We examine our drinking water with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle This experiment shows how different the drinking water from different places in the same region can be. The comparison of tap water with bottled mineral water and distilled water leads to surprising findings. Benefits experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks measuring and comparing the conductivity values of tap water samples from different locations comparison with commercial drinking water discussion of the causes of conductivity values and their differences Learning Objectives Water quality ion content of water salt content (salinity)

name: We investigate our physical fitness with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿Principle With the help of an electrocardiogram (ECG) the sum of the electrical activities of all myocardial fibres can be registered. The heart activity increases with the load on the body to maintain the stability of the cardiovascular system. The contraction of the heart cannot be deliberately controlled by man. This experiment investigates how physical stress affects heart activity. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks generate an ECG in the transition from a state of rest to a state of physical exertion (20 knee bends) generate an ECG under load in transition to a resting state and calculate the time in which the resting pulse is reached again Learning Objectives Electrocardiogram (ECG) strain ECG heart activity heart muscle

name: We visit a wastewater treatment plant with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle When visiting the sewage treatment plant with its fascinating processes and cleaning methods, we are not content with sensual impressions alone, but deepen our understanding of the microbiological processes by measuring the most important parameters: temperature, pH and salt content. Benefits experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increased media competence. Tasks visit of a sewage treatment plant and sampling examination of microbiological processes examination of the different process steps in a sewage treatment plant and comparison of the measured values Learning Objectives Wastewater Water purification Nitrification Denitrification sedimentation

name: Weather observation with Cobra SMARTsense
description: Principle The weather is primarily influenced by the parameters air pressure, humidity, air temperature and brightness. With the Cobra SMARTsense Weatherstation it is possible to set up your own weather station to measure these parameters in order to make the relationships between them understandable. Tasks Measurement of air pressure, humidity, air temperature, light intensity on different days of the year and at different times of day Discussion of the dependence between the individual weather parameters Learning Objective Students should become familiar with weather parameters, link them to practical measurements and be able to establish connections between important weather and light parameters. Benefits Experiment is part of a complete solution with numerous experiments in the field of ecology Measurements possible indoors and outdoors, for mobile use Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life, etc.) including protocol questions Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science lessons with tablets or smartphones Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP Increased media competence

name: Weight with Cobra SMARTsense
description: ﻿Principle "A mass is not only inert, but also heavy." This means that not only is a force necessary to accelerate a mass, but that every mass in the Earth's gravity field also experiences a force, the weight force. Benefits Especially understandable and didactically prepared description of the experiment (relevance to everyday life etc.) incl. protocol questions. Future-oriented teaching: Integration into digital science teaching with tablets or smartphones. Increased motivation of students by using the intuitive measureAPP. Increase of media competence Tasks Measure with the force sensor the weight force which the slotted weights experience in the earth's gravitational field. Determine the relationship between the mass of the slotted weights and the weight force acting on them. Learning Objectives The students should understand the difference between a mass and its weight.

name: pH value tests on succulent plants with Cobra SMARTsense
description: (no description provided)

name: pH-value of everyday substances (cleaning agents, food) with Cobra SMARTsense
description: PrincipleIn this experiment, students are introduced to the basics of acids and bases using everyday examples. In addition to practical work in the laboratory, the significance of different pH values is also discussed. Tasks Handling and, if necessary, calibrating a pH meter (Cobra SMARTsense pH) Measuring the pH values of various acids and bases from everyday life, e.g. sparkling water, milk, lemonade or lemon juice Classification of the pH values into the "acidic" or "alkaline" environment Assignment of the environments to discrete pH value ranges Learning objectives The students will find out what a pH meter measures and how everyday chemicals react in water. In this context the terms "acidic" or "basic" environment are clarified and they are related to the concentration of protons (hydronium ions) in water. Benefits Digital learning thanks to the free measureAPP: display of measurement data on the tablet and option to export for evaluationÂ to a computer Minimal preparation time: experimental literature available for students and teachers Simple teaching and efficient learning when using the interactive experiment literature Risk assessment available for students and teachers

name: Coulomb potential and Coulomb field of metal spheres
description: Principle Conducting spheres with different diameters are charged electrically. The static potentials and the accompanying electric field intensities are computer-assisted determined by means of an electric field meter with a potential measuring probe, as a function of position and voltage. Benefits Supplies non-hazardous high voltage Loss-less measurement of electric field strength High-sensitive electric field meter also suitable for electrostatic measurement of voltages Tasks For a conducting sphere of diameter 2R = 12 cm, electrostatic potential is determined as a function of voltage at a constant distance from the surface of the sphere. For the conducting spheres of diameters 2R = 12 cm and 2R = 4 cm, electrostatic potential at constant voltage is determined as a function of the distance from the surface of the sphere. For both conducting spheres, electricfield strength is determined as a function of charging voltage at three different distances from the surface of the sphere. For the conducting sphere of diameter 2R = 12 cm, electric field strength is determined as a function of the distance from the surface of the sphere at constant charging voltage. Learning objectives Electric field Field intensity Electric flow Electric charge Gaussian rule Surface charge density Induction Induction constant Capacitance Gradient Image charge Electrostatic potential Potential difference

name: Detection of discontinuities
description: Principle The experiment demonstrates the application and performance of various non-destructive test methods with the aid of ultrasound. A test object with different types of discontinuities is used to perform various detection methods. First, the test object is scanned in order to determine which detection method is suitable for which type of defect. Then, the signal-to-noise-ratio is determined for each discontinuity with a straight beam probe, angle beam probe, and a transmitter-receiver probe (TR probe). This is followed by a discussion of the results in view of the selection of the most suitable detection method for a specific task. Benefits Complete echoscopy system incl. test objects to perform different NDT techniques With the same equipment angle beam inspection and TOFD can also be performed Display of measurement values as with a diagnostic system Tasks Use a 2 MHz straight beam probe (vertical scanning direction) to define the suitable device parameters at the ultrasonic echoscope. Produce a B-scan of the test object. Assign the various defects (discontinuities) of the test object to the B-scan. Then, determine the signal-to-noise-ratio for each discontinuity in the A-mode. Repeat the above-mentioned tests/measurements on the same test object with an angle beam probe and then with a TR probe (transmitter-receiver probe). Assess and represent the detectability of the discontinuities with the respective test methods. Learning objectives Ultrasonic echography Discontinuity A-Mode Straight beam probe Angle beam probe Reflection Beam angle Signal-to-noise ratio

name: Energy loss of alpha particles in gases with MCA
description: Principle A study is made of the connection between the energy E of alpha-particles and the path x travelled by them in air at standard pressure. The measurements recorded enable the differencial energy loss dE/dx to be calculated as a function of x. Tasks The spectrum of a covered 241Am source is measured at a fixed distance s as a function of the pressure p. The distance s is selected in such a way as to correspond to the maximum range at the highest pressure measurable with the manometer used. The energy corresponding to the central points of the individual spectra are determined (after calibration of the measurement layout with an open 241Am-emitter, see 3.) and plotted as a function of the distance x converted to a 1013 hPa basis. Using this function, the differential energy loss (dE/dx) is then calculated as a function of x and again plotted on the graph. The spectrum of the source used in 1 is measured initially under the same geometric conditions under vacuum and subsequently with the vessel filled with helium, nitrogen or carbon dioxide, in each case under identical pressures. The different energy loss values are compared with the electron concentration in the particular gas. The mean energy with which the alpha-particles leave the covered americium source is determined by calibration against the open americium emitter (E = 5.485 MeV). (This value is required for the evaluation in 1.) Learning objectives Range Range dispersion Mean free path length Mean ionization energy of gas atoms Mean energy loss of a-particles per collision Differencial energy loss Bethe formula Electron concentration in gases

name: Faraday effect
description: Principle The angle of rotation of the polarization- plane of plane polarised light through a flint glass rod is found to be a linear function of the product of the mean flux-densitiy and the length of the optical medium. The factor of proportionally, called Verdet's constant, is investigated as a function of the wavelength and the optical medium.
